terms.definition: A function L(ŷ, y) scoring how wrong one prediction ŷ is versus true target y; training minimizes its average over labeled examples.
Regression loss (y − ŷ)² per example; batch MSE is the average squared residual. Heavily penalizes large errors.
Regression loss |y − ŷ| per example; batch MAE is the average absolute residual. More robust to outliers than MSE.
Classification loss −y log ŷ − (1−y) log(1−ŷ) for binary labels and sigmoid probability ŷ; Bernoulli form of H(P, Q).
Multi-class loss −log ŷ_y for one-hot true class y and softmax probabilities ŷ; standard single-label classification objective.
Average loss (1/n) Σ L(ŷᵢ, yᵢ) over the training set; the quantity gradient descent minimizes.
Synonym for cross-entropy loss in classification; negative log-likelihood of the true label under predicted probabilities.
